Note.Para: The WINDSHEAR PREDICTIVE L(R) status message will be displayed after turning inoperative weather radar OFF. The airplane must also be dispatched using MEL 34-42-01-01A.

OperationNote.Para: Set the inoperative weather radar OFF using the Tuning and Control Panel (TCP) SYS POWER page.

ListItem.Para: At least one weather radar must be operative for EDTO operations.
Reactive windshear alert (GPWS mode 7) operates normally.
Weather must be suitable for flight without weather radar.
Alternate procedures are established and used.

The aircraft must not depart if the weather radar is unserviceable and weather forecasts indicate the probability of thunderstorms or cloud formations associated with severe turbulence anywhere along the route to be flown, including the route to a planned alternate.

If during the flight the weather radar system becomes unserviceable, the flight may continue provided that penetration of any cloud formation likely to be associated with severe turbulence is avoided.
Review FCOM supplementary procedure for windshear.
If both weather radar systems are inoperative, EDTO operations are restricted to 120 minutes.

Note.Para: The WINDSHEAR SYS advisory message will be displayed.

ListItem.Para: At least one weather radar must be operative for EDTO operations beyond 120 minutes.
Weather must be suitable for flight without weather radar.
Alternate procedures are established and used.

The aircraft must not depart if the weather radar is unserviceable and weather forecasts indicate the probability of thunderstorms or cloud formations associated with severe turbulence anywhere along the route to be flown, including the route to a planned alternate.

If during the flight the weather radar system becomes unserviceable, the flight may continue provided that penetration of any cloud formation likely to be associated with severe turbulence is avoided.
Review FCOM supplementary procedure for windshear and QRH Manoeuvres Windshear guidance.
PM must maintain a heightened awareness of the flight path and performance of the aircraft during takeoff, approach and landing and alert the PF if flight parameters are out of normal tolerance.
Be especially alert for and announce:
Excessive terrain closure rate.
Excessive descent rate.
Altitude loss after takeoff or go-around.
Unsafe terrain clearance.
Deviation from glideslope.
If both weather radar systems are inoperative, EDTO operations are restricted to 120 minutes.

Note.Para: The WINDSHEAR SYS advisory message will be displayed.

ListItem.Para: Reactive windshear alert (GPWS mode 7) operates normally.
Operations procedure is used.

OperationProcedure.Para: Review FCOM supplementary procedure for windshear.

Note.Para: The WINDSHEAR SYS advisory message will be displayed.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are established and used.

ListItem.Para: Review FCOM supplementary procedure for windshear and QRH Manoeuvres Windshear guidance.
PM must maintain a heightened awareness of the flight path and performance of the aircraft during takeoff, approach and landing and alert the PF if flight parameters are out of normal tolerance.
Be especially alert for and announce:
Excessive terrain closure rate.
Excessive descent rate.
Altitude loss after takeoff or go-around.
Unsafe terrain clearance.
Deviation from glideslope.

Note.Para: The WINDSHEAR SYS advisory message will be displayed.

OperationNote.Para: Manual TILT adjustments may be necessary to optimize weather display.
